leadText: This article is about the move Tackle. For the field move, see Tackle (Field Move). Tackle (Japanese: たいあたり Body Blow) is a damage-dealing Normal-type move introduced in Generation I.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation III to IV 1.2 Generations V and VI 1.3 Generation VII onwards 1.4 Generation VIII 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.5 Generation IX 1.5.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Tackle inflicts damage and has no secondary effect. Generation III to IV [ edit | edit source ] Tackle can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 4 bonus appeal points if Defense Curl , Harden , or Leer was used in the previous turn. Generations V and VI [ edit | edit source ] Tackle's power was increased from 35 to 50 and its accuracy from 95% to 100%. Generation VII onwards [ edit | edit source ] Tackle's power was decreased from 50 to 40. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Tackle's PP was reduced from 35 to 30. D P Pt HG SS PBR A physical attack in which the user charges and slams into the foe with its whole body. sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Unlike other generations, all of the Unova first partner Pokémon are able to learn Tackle. Due to Pound and Scratch being absent from Pokémon Legends: Arceus and Pokémon Legends: Z-A , all Kanto, Johto, Sinnoh, and Kalos first partner Pokémon have also been able to learn Tackle at some point. In the animated series , Tackle is the most common move to be used by Pokémon that cannot learn it in the games . Tackle is the most learned move that isn't taught by TMs , TRs , or Move Tutors . It is also the most learned move at Level 1. In several games from Generation I onwards, the Pokédex states that Rhyhorn has powerful tackles. However, Rhyhorn was unable to learn Tackle until Generation VIII . This was first said in Rhyhorn's Pokédex entry from Japanese Pokémon Red and Green , although this entry was left untranslated until it was reused in Pokémon FireRed . Rhyhorn's Japanese Pokédex entries correctly use Tackle's Japanese name 「たいあたり」, or its equivalent 「体当た」 in kanji mode.
